reasoning: A US ally (NATO member or Major Non-NATO Ally) independently acquiring a nuclear weapon would be a massive geopolitical event. Historical base rates for nuclear proliferation are exceedingly low, and acquiring a nuke requires significant time, material, and often visible testing. While there has been much debate in 2024 and 2025 about countries like South Korea, Japan, and Poland exploring nuclear options due to shifting US political dynamics, multiple reliable sources up to November 2025 confirm that none have actually developed or acquired their own nuclear weapons. Sources still list the same 9 nuclear-armed states (US, Russia, UK, France, China, India, Pakistan, Israel, North Korea). The UK, France, Israel, and Pakistan already possessed nuclear weapons well before the March 10, 2025 cutoff, ruling them out. There are no credible reports of any other US ally independently acquiring full control of a nuclear weapon in 2025.
